label: true-dup
a: Policy: all background jobs must be idempotent. Running a job twice with the same payload must produce the same outcome as running it once. This is required because RabbitMQ guarantees at-least-once delivery.
b: Background job handlers are required to be idempotent — executing the same job twice must not cause double-effects. The message queue delivers at-least-once, not exactly-once. Each handler must use one of: idempotency keys checked against the database, unique constraints that reject duplicate inserts, or check-then-skip logic.
reviewer_notes: Both state the same idempotent-jobs policy driven by at-least-once delivery.
